market_overview.headline: Market structure showing fragility with both SPY and QQQ displaying narrow EMA spreads and bearish cross risks despite Full Bull signals.
market_overview.detail: SPY at 708 and QQQ at 651 both maintain Full Bull structure but with concerning EMA compression — SPY's 8W only 0.46% above 13W and QQQ's 8W just 0.78% above 13W, signaling potential momentum loss. BTC remains in Bear Rally mode, creating mixed risk-on signals.

reversal_candidates.headline: Bear Rally Reversal Setups (Bullish Crossover Potential)

items.name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
items.note: Mega-cap tech showing strength 4% above both 8W and 13W with tight EMA convergence signaling potential reversal.

items.name: Bank of America Corporation
items.crossover_detail: 8W just below 13W (0.03%) — bullish cross potential; 13W just below 21W (0.15%) — bullish cross potential
items.note: Banking giant with double crossover potential as EMAs compress — watch for sector rotation into financials.

items.name: BlackRock, Inc.
items.note: Asset manager showing 3.8% strength above 8W with EMA convergence amid potential financial sector revival.

items.name: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.
items.note: EDA software leader rallying 6.7% above 8W with tight EMA structure suggesting tech reversal potential.

items.name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
items.note: Energy giant's low volume pullback suggests orderly selling — quality entry in Iran war beneficiary.

items.name: Chevron Corporation
items.note: Another major oil play with low volume selling — both XOM and CVX showing disciplined pullbacks.

items.name: Cheniere Energy, Inc.
items.note: LNG export leader pulling back on normal volume — Iran conflict supporting energy infrastructure plays.

items.name: Corteva, Inc.
items.note: Agricultural chemical play with low volume pullback — food security themes amid global conflicts.

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic/Storage Tech Leading Momentum
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology sector dominates momentum leaders with storage and semiconductor names showing massive acceleration. Data storage revival theme (WDC, STX, SNDK) and optical networking (LITE, CIEN) driving tech momentum outside mega-caps.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy/Materials vs Tech Divergence

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score driven by Iran war premium and supply disruption fears.
Industrial strength supporting infrastructure and defense spending themes.
Tech hardware showing momentum acceleration despite broader tech weakness.

weakest.note: Software and services lagging badly as growth concerns mount.
Biotech and medical device weakness continuing regulatory overhang.
Banking sector mixed despite some reversal candidates emerging.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from growth/tech services into energy/materials/hardware. Iran war driving energy outperformance while tech services face valuation compression.

items.text: Both SPY and QQQ showing EMA compression with bearish cross risks despite bull signals
Iran conflict escalation creating oil price shock hitting airlines and consumer spending
Technology services sector showing broad deterioration with 78% bearish readings
BTC bear structure diverging from equity strength suggests risk-off undercurrents

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iran war escalation dominated headlines with reports of air defense engagement in Tehran and shipping disruptions in Hormuz Strait. Energy sector benefited from supply disruption fears while airlines faced fuel cost pressures. Mixed earnings reports and geopolitical uncertainty drove defensive positioning despite some corporate pricing deal announcements.

items.text: Iran war escalation with air defense activity in Tehran boosting oil prices and energy stocks
Shipping disruptions in Strait of Hormuz adding supply chain risk premium to markets
Airlines facing fuel shock from war premium despite record demand according to Reuters
S&P Global survey showing business activity recovery but war-driven price increases
Regeneron pricing deal with Trump administration providing sector-specific relief
